commit.message:
fix: session resume failure and agent status tracking (#164)

Fixes #162

## Changes

### Timeout Removal
- **AcpAdapter.ts**: Removed 10-minute `PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS` that killed
long-running agents. 30-minute AlertEngine still provides observability.
- **AcpAdapter.ts**: Removed 30s `SDK_TIMEOUT_MS` wrapper from
`loadSession()` — it was killing session resume for non-trivial
sessions. Other SDK calls (spawnAndConnect, newSession) retain the 30s
timeout.
- **AgentAcpBridge.ts**: Removed dead `prompt_timeout` listener (event
no longer emitted).

### Agent Status Tracking
- **WebSocketServer.ts**: Removed 500ms WS status throttle — all status
transitions now broadcast immediately for instant UI updates.
- **AgentAcpBridge.ts**: Added `_notifyStatusChange()` call when agent
starts running.

### Timer Race Condition
- **AgentManager.ts**: 30s deletion timer now checks agent instance
identity before deleting, preventing replacement agents from being
removed. Added comment explaining why `dispose()` runs outside the
identity guard.

### Event-Driven Task Delivery
- **AgentAcpBridge.ts**: Replaced `setTimeout`-based task delivery with
event-driven `queueMessage()` pattern for more reliable task delivery to
agents.

### Session Resume
- **AcpAdapter.ts**: `loadSession()` failure intentionally throws to
surface errors to the UI. The `session_resume_failed` event is emitted
for observability before throwing.
- **projects.ts**: Removed redundant `buildBriefing()` call from resume
path — resume gets full context from `loadSession()` conversation
replay.

### Context Compression
- **AgentManager.ts**: Send command reference reminder when context
compression fires. Agents lose knowledge of available commands after
compression, so this is the right time to remind them.

### Documentation Updates
- **adapter-architecture-pattern skill**: Updated session resume docs —
no longer mentions fallback to `newSession()`, now documents
throw-on-failure behavior.
- **DETAILED_EVENT_REFERENCE.md**: Updated `agent:status` throttle docs.

## Testing
- All tests pass
- TypeScript compiles clean
- Full test suite: 3918 tests pass (174 files)

BLE: fix distance and duration parsing for GPS outdoor runs

- field11.f4 = total distance in meters (GPS runs)
- field7.f1  = accurate duration in seconds (vs f13 which is rounded minutes)

Confirmed from March 12 run hex decode:

commit.message:
fix(core-infra): add explicit Tags to RedisSubnetGroup, fix ManagedBy on all resources

RedisSubnetGroup was the only resource in core-infrastructure.yaml without an
explicit Tags: property. CloudFormation propagates stack-level tags to untagged
resources via a separate AddTagsToResource API call, which the ElastiCache
SubnetGroup handler rejects with a tag validation error.

Adding explicit Tags: inline ensures CloudFormation includes them directly in
the CreateCacheSubnetGroup API call instead of a separate tag update, fixing:
  'Tag values may only contain unicode letters, digits, whitespace, or
   one of these symbols: _ . : / = + \ - @'

Also globally fixed ManagedBy tag value from 'CloudFormation' to 'github-actions'
on all 18 resources in the template for consistency with other workflows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: arrow key navigation via UIViewRepresentable pressesBegan

Previous attempts failed:
- onKeyPress: requires UIKit first-responder; GeometryReader (CarouselView
  root) cannot become first responder, so events never arrive.
- keyboardShortcut: SwiftUI collapses zero-sized host views on Mac Catalyst
  and strips them from the effective responder chain.

New approach: KeyboardNavigationHandler (UIViewRepresentable) creates an
ArrowKeyView that:
  - canBecomeFirstResponder = true
  - calls becomeFirstResponder() in didMoveToWindow (deferred to next
    run-loop so SwiftUI layout finishes first)
  - overrides pressesBegan to detect UIKeyboardHIDUsage.keyboardLeftArrow
    / keyboardRightArrow and invoke SwiftUI callbacks
  - re-acquires first responder in updateUIView whenever SwiftUI re-renders
    (covers sheet dismiss and other state transitions)

pressesBegan is the lowest-level UIKit mechanism; it works on Mac Catalyst
and iPad with hardware keyboard without any focus requirements.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: arrow key navigation using keyboardShortcut instead of onKeyPress

onKeyPress requires the view to hold UIKit first-responder focus.
GeometryReader (the root of CarouselView) cannot become first responder,
so onKeyPress never fired regardless of @FocusState setup.

keyboardShortcut creates a UIKeyCommand processed by the UIKit responder
chain — no view focus required. Two zero-sized, invisible (opacity:0)
buttons with .leftArrow/.rightArrow shortcuts are added to the carousel
section. Text fields consume arrow keys first via the responder chain,
so carousel navigation only fires when no text field is active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(aws02): add pre-flight stack state check to detect ROLLBACK_COMPLETE before deploy

Before running CloudFormation deploy, check if the core-infra stack exists
and is in a deployable state. If it is ROLLBACK_COMPLETE or CREATE_FAILED,
fail immediately with a clear message directing the developer to delete
the stuck stack from the AWS Console before retrying.

This prevents the confusing 'parameter value does not exist' CloudFormation
error that occurs when a previous failed run left the stack in ROLLBACK_COMPLETE
state (subnet IDs were valid but CloudFormation rejects deploying to that state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: auto-extract ingredients text on photo/PDF import using Vision

When the user imports an ingredients photo (from library or camera) or a
PDF, the app now automatically runs text extraction in the background:
- Photo: Vision OCR (VNRecognizeTextRequest, accurate mode)
- PDF: PDFKit text extraction (existing TextExtractor)

The extracted text is written to recipe.ingredientsText and the type
selector is switched to .text so the user immediately sees the result
in the editable text field. The original photo/PDF is kept in its own
field so it can still be shared/re-extracted later.

A 'Extracting text with Vision…' progress banner appears in the
ingredients section while extraction is in progress.

commit.message:
fix: remove invalid $device_id='all' guard from latency panel SQL

Using 'AND ($device_id = 'all' OR ...)' breaks PostgreSQL because
Grafana substitutes the variable unquoted — pi03-lan = 'all' is not
a valid boolean expression. Use device_id = ANY(ARRAY[$device_id]::text[])
consistently (matching other dashboards). Also set multi:true on the
variable so the All option expands correctly.

commit.message:
Daily Code Quality - Test coverage for resource generator commands (#3083)

* test(gen): add execution tests for resource generator commands

Add snapshot-based execution tests for 12 kubectl-backed resource
generator commands in pkg/cli/cmd/workload/gen/. Each test exercises
the full command execution pipeline (createGenCmd → kubectl dry-run
client → YAML output) and captures the result as a go-snaps snapshot.

Coverage added:
- Namespace, ConfigMap, Deployment, Job, CronJob
- Ingress (simple, TLS via rule, multiple rules)
- PodDisruptionBudget, PriorityClass, ResourceQuota
- RoleBinding, ClusterRoleBinding, ServiceAccount
- Service (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName)
- Secret (generic, TLS, docker-registry)

Also includes gen_snapshots.go (build-ignored) that documents how
snapshots are regenerated when kubectl version changes.

Role and ClusterRole are intentionally excluded: their create commands
perform server-side API group discovery even with --dry-run=client,
which requires an active cluster not available in unit test environments.
